text:
Market today: Mixed/cautious mood. The big mover for your book is Samsung and SK Hynix tumbling 9%+ - that's a chip rout spreading from Wall Street, and it directly pressures your semiconductor-heavy names. No clean macro catalyst driving things higher today.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the one screaming for attention - it's a 3X leveraged chip ETF already deep in the red, stop at $200, and a broader chip selloff is exactly the environment where this thing accelerates lower fast. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand new, very volatile, and already down hard - stop at $21.38 is razor-thin, margin amplifies any gap down. SMH (-2.5%) is your cleaner chip exposure but it's bleeding too.

Watch: 1) Whether the chip selloff (Samsung/Hynix leading) bleeds into US names at open - SMH breaking toward $540 stop is your warning. 2) SPAL's $21.38 stop - it's essentially right there.

Bottom line: Protect capital first - your leveraged positions are your biggest risk today, not your next buy.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your line - chip rout is live today. If it gaps toward that level, don't hesitate, cut it. Leveraged decay plus a down sector is a brutal combo on margin.
SPAL: Stop at $21.38 is basically right here. New leveraged ETF already -13.72% - if it opens weak, respect the stop immediately, no second-guessing.
No new buys today - chip sector is under active pressure from the Samsung/Hynix rout. Let the dust settle before adding exposure.
